concepts.title: Basic properties of complex numbers
concepts.conceptBody:
Some properties of imaginary numbers 

  • If $a\And b$ are positive real numbers, then $\sqrt{-a}\times \sqrt{-b}=-\sqrt{ab}$
  • $\sqrt{\text{a}}\times \sqrt{\text{b}}=\sqrt{\text{ab}}$ is not valid if both $\text{a}\And \text{ }\!\!~\!\!\text{ b}$ are negative. This is valid only when at least one of $a$ or $b$ is positive or zero.
  • The sum of four consecutive powers of $i$ is zero.

$\text{ }\!\!~\!\!\text{ i}\text{.e}\text{. }\!\!~\!\!\text{ }{{i}^{n}}+{{i}^{n+1}}+{{i}^{n+2}}+{{i}^{n+3}}=0,\left( n\in I \right)$

Complex Numbers 

If two numbers $a,b$ are real, then a number $a+ib$ will be a complex number. here, $a$ is the real part represented by $\text{Re}\left( \text{z} \right)\And \text{ }\!\!~\!\!\text{ b}$ is the imaginary part $\text{Im}\left( \text{z} \right)$

where $z=a+ib$

for ex. $z=5-3i$, then $\text{Re}\left( z \right)=5$, $\text{Im}\left( \text{z} \right)=-3$

If for $z=a+ib$

  • $\text{Im}\left( z \right)$ is zero, then the no. is purely real, & if Re (z) is zero, then the no. is purely imaginary.
  • Like we represent real numbers by the set $\text{R}$, complex numbers are denoted by the set C.

Note: Real numbers are subsets of complex numbers.

i.e., every real number is a complex number.
